title: What sanctions are actually designed to do - Sarah Paine
summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Sarah Paine on the design and function of economic sanctions as a geopolitical tool. The discussion explores the actual mechanisms and objectives behind sanction regimes rather than their stated purposes.
writeup: Sarah Paine examines economic sanctions as a policy instrument, separating their actual design and purposes from the public rationales offered for them. The discussion matters because the stated aims of sanctions often differ from what they are built to accomplish. The takeaway is a framing of sanctions in terms of their intended functions rather than their official justifications.
